summary: Comprehensive coverage of all task requirements (cross-product, include/exclude, max-parallel, fail-fast, validation, error handling) across 24+ unit tests and integration tests running through act. Unit tests are well-structured with clear fixtures and independent assertions. Rigor is strong for happy path and basic error cases, though some boundary conditions (single-element axes, duplicates in includes, exact max_combinations limit) lack explicit tests. Design is mixed: matrix-generator.bats is clean and maintainable; run-act-tests.bats introduces fragility via the extract_section() awk parser which depends on exact output formatting with section markers—any workflow output format change breaks many tests in opaque ways. Fixture files (test/fixtures/*.json) are referenced but not provided, making it impossible to verify test data correctness. The integration test approach is sound (act execution, output capture to act-result.txt, exact value assertions) but the parsing strategy is brittle. Would catch most regressions in core functionality, but integration test maintenance would be a concern if output format ever changes.
